76460.subject: Onchain Assets Weekly: 7/24/26
76460.body:
Onchain Assets Weekly

Автор: Securitize


Автор: Securitize

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/onchain-assets-weekly-72426-securitize-dbggc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B4seueh5ETLqFjIDNTul%2BVw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2guXRDoifdXsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrzby95p~wb-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrzby95p-wb
  
This week’s highlights: Securitize recognized by CNBC; Carlos Domingo joins Bankless; Uniswap Permissioned Pools; and BNY Treasury rails go 24/7. Securitize Named to CNBC's World's Top Fintech Companies of 2026 | CNBC CNBC and Statista included Securitize among 40 digital assets companies recognized globally as blockchain infrastructure and tokenization services become more institutionalized. READ MORE Carlos Domingo on Securitize’s Business, Tokenization Demand and Tokenized Stocks | Bankless Carlos Domingo joins Bankless to discuss Securitize’s public listing, real vs. synthetic tokenized stocks, and why demand and liquidity remain tokenization’s biggest hurdles. WATCH NOW Uniswap Pushes Deeper Into Tokenized Assets With Permissioned Trading Pools | CoinDesk The framework, developed with Superstate, Securitize and Dowgo, lets regulated funds and securities trade on Uniswap while enforcing compliance rules necessary for institutions. READ MORE BNY Pushes Toward 24/7 Treasury Settlement as Tokenization Grows | Bloomberg BNY’s push for “always-on” Treasury settlement shows how tokenization and stablecoin activity are beginning to influence the operating hours of traditional market infrastructure. READ MORE
Приєднатися до обговорення
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/onchain-assets-weekly-72426-securitize-dbggc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B4seueh5ETLqFjIDNTul%2BVw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2guXRDoifdXsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mrzby95p~wb-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrzby95p-wb
  
      

----------------------------------------

Це повідомлення е-поштою адресовано учасникові Oleh Novosadov (I am 36 years old, and I am passionate about the world of cryptocurrencies and blockchain technologies. My professional interests include investing in cryptocurrencies, exploring blockchain, Web3, and the DeFi sector.)
Дізнатися, чому ми включили цю інформацію https://www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin/answer/4788?lang=uk&lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B4seueh5ETLqFjIDNTul%2BVw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2guXRDoifdXsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-SecurityHelp-0-textfooterglimmer&trkEmail=

76458.subject: The AI cold war turns hot
76458.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-cold-war-turns-hot-genai-works-nmchc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B2TK25GKPTYStsLr86YBEtQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3IORoHS1FpXsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrz7sisz~yl-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrz7sisz-yl
  
Welcome back! This week the gloves came off: the White House accused Kimi's maker of copying Anthropic, two rivals may share a data center, Substack started sniffing for AI writing and an OpenAI model hacked its way to an answer key.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: The US accuses Kimi's maker of distilling Anthropic's Fable, and sanctions are on the table Meta may lease $10B of AI compute to Anthropic, its direct rival Substack ships an AI detector to catch "Claudefishing" An OpenAI model escaped its sandbox and hacked Hugging Face to steal a test's answers PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. The US just accused Kimi's maker of stealing from Anthropic, and sanctions are on the table The White House escalated the AI cold war this week.  Trump's science and technology adviser Michael Kratsios publicly accused Moonshot AI, the Chinese startup behind Kimi K3, of a "large scale" effort to steal the capabilities of top US models.  The method he named is distillation, where a weaker model extracts answers from a stronger one to copy its behavior, and he says Moonshot distilled from Anthropic's Fable.  He also claims Moonshot trained on restricted Nvidia GB300 servers it wasn't supposed to reach. The business stakes are what make this more than a diplomatic spat.  Kimi K3 stunned the industry last week by appearing to close the gap with Western frontier models, and it goes fully open-source on July 27, the first model of its scale anyone can download and customize.  If the capability that impressed everyone was partly distilled from US models, the competitive story shifts from "China caught up" to "China copied," which changes how enterprises and investors weigh Chinese open models. The response is hardening into policy.  Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said sanctions "will be on the table" when Chinese firms cross into "industrial-scale distillation attacks," adding that "open source is not open season on American IP."  This follows Anthropic's June accusation that Alibaba illicitly extracted its Claude capabilities the same way, and the White House's April pledge to help US labs fight "industrial-scale campaigns" to steal their technology. China rejected the framing, with its foreign ministry calling it the "politicizing" of trade and tech and pointing to "joint contr

76457.subject:
Our First Starcloud Offering Was a $2.9M Sellout. What Drove the
 Demand?
76457.body:
Another chance for exposure to the company sending AI data centers into orbit

private-header-1-Jul-23-2026-07-10-27-1765-PM (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VWzCyy8x03nDW71WhjB3JC0WSW8wLjj95RTY07N7lCf0v3pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3pZW7Xksj388hhBpN8KHkT1JVKllW1KxKqC3qwBzbW40fJ3R385ggVW7dHkrw4Zg2bwV8G9n62BhPDHW15dhD23vM_XnW4_2L8R7958RcW8LTwjl44PljfW2vQB6Q77j2KVW6kRrM-3WTXWHV17GWB3kZnVLW3VyV6H5LWsp6M-W4stGD0QWW2msfzb2R3YPfW7cSLkB67gmjMW434fvp4S5dgdW560sjN5pCCl9W72d6RV2_922gW6xlsMN8CKyV7W1ZMFhx3Hkx4dW7zqpdg5ff76YV7k0kX41HJjYN3P__3hYM6rWW1dHpqZ970L1TW2ss9gv248jGFVDV3mx3YtBy-W4GXzCf2ZhH25f6fbNlW04 )

Hi Oleg,

Series Starcloud-QP-1 was a $2.9 million sellout on StartEngine.

What drove the demand?

The company’s solar-powered Starcloud 1 was the first to train a model in space, run a version of Gemini, and execute high-powered inference on satellite imagery.¹

CEO Philip Johnston told CNN that his goal is simple: build a sustainable solution to unlock the “future that we've been promised by AI,” from drug discovery to self-driving cars and beyond.¹

Starcloud is working closely with heavy hitters like NVIDIA, SpaceX, and Starlink to make that vision a reality.²

Some high-profile investors have been paying attention, too:

-

One of the first (if not the first) to market with space-based data centers.³

-

Est. $1.1B valuation, the fastest Y Combinator grad to pass $1B.⁴

-

$193M+ raised to date from a16z, Google, Sequoia, and more.⁵

-

Targeting a new $200M+ round that would double its valuation.⁴

There’s no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow for Starcloud, or that the company will be profitable. See the risk disclosures on the offering page.

With Series Starcloud-QP-2 (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VWzCyy8x03nDW71WhjB3JC0WSW8wLjj95RTY07N7lCf0v3pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3pZW7Xksj388hhBpN8KHkT1JVKllW1KxKqC3qwBzbW40fJ3R385ggVW7dHkrw4Zg2bwV8G9n62BhPDHW15dhD23vM_XnW4_2L8R7958RcW8LTwjl44PljfW2vQB6Q77j2KVW6kRrM-3WTXWHV17GWB3kZnVLW3VyV6H5LWsp6M-W4stGD0QWW2msfzb2R3YPfW7cSLkB67gmjMW434fvp4S5dgdW560sjN5pCCl9W72d6RV2_922gW6xlsMN8CKyV7W1ZMFhx3Hkx4dW7zqpdg5ff76YV7k0kX41HJjYN3P__3hYM6rWW1dHpqZ970L1TW2ss9gv248jGFVDV3mx3YtBy-W4GXzCf2ZhH25f6fbNlW04 ) , qualified purchasers can once again gain exposure to a company aiming to reimagine the future of AI infrastructure by taking computing to a new frontier.

View Opportunity
(https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VWzCyy8x03nDW71WhjB3JC0WSW8wLjj95RTY07N7lCf0v3pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3pZW7Xksj388hhBpN8KHkT1JVKllW1KxKqC3qwBzbW40fJ3R385ggVW7dHkrw4Zg2bwV8G9n62BhPDHW15dhD23vM_XnW4_2L8R7958RcW8LTwjl44PljfW2vQB6Q77j2KVW6kRrM-3WTXWHV17GWB3kZnVLW3VyV6H5LWsp6M-W4stGD0QWW2msfzb2R3YPfW7cSLkB67gmjMW434fvp4S5dgdW560sjN5pCCl9W72d6RV2_922gW6xlsMN8CKyV7W1ZMFhx3Hkx4dW7zqpdg5ff76YV7k0kX41HJjYN3P__3hYM6rWW1dHpqZ970L1TW2ss9gv248jGFVDV3mx3YtBy-W4GXzCf2ZhH25f6fbNlW04 )

Investment is in Series 87-2 (“Series Starcloud-QP-2”), whic

76452.subject: The surprising benefits of red light
76452.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDd9AAcAAAAABGcWtM-aeIdvAhbqErV5iqq10b_rI3mKpCvJpUdt2_u35fw7b-G6gbt5ZjeRMI7O91urLGU0YMoGAThZsLpoWU1Y04zUPWjgDEnQZJnQhaZM 

July 24th 2026

 


  
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDd9AAcAAAAABGcWtM-bgU5zO1v7W6kq9J35kG7wb8mqXbVPVJR_AppqBkF8D0CmGMQsPAojUPSiMjHZLmsw8lxMPhklyVvmdmT6u-qkNetYu0OWZzgZ2naX 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDd9AAcAAAAABGcWtM-cbDZT6KrVJcZ-XquKMJKU1-cqi5BN5ydfcoUnndMRldsQU9UaAsUcnMu7u9XcXGrdSSN3dLyuHIMcBtdqsnmW1x1RHdbHWPxl7-HT 


 


 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



 I scoffed at my partner for buying a faddish red-light mask last year (though his skin undoubtedly looks fresher than mine). Perhaps I was hasty. There is evidence that photobiomodulation, as therapies that use red and infrared light are known, help to treat traumatic brain injuries, Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s diseases and 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDd9AAcAAAAABGcWtM-dA6g-stPNV7pLb7nZQqbipcFBZXGMyqegO1h1OGurVw6lmL474C5q_0s90X3o2s4u_oqhi9K4JEEe1cb2hhwo4lECnUI83oc 
even psychiatric disorders .
 


 Gen Z’s career challenges have prompted a rash of advice books. Our culture section finds that two recent ones demonstrate why successful people make bad career advisers. But one takes an un
